chicken stir-fry with rice.name: Chicken Stir-fry with Rice
ingredients: 1 lb boneless, skinless chicken breast or thighs, cut into 1-inch pieces
2 tbsp soy sauce (or tamari for gluten-free), divided
1 tbsp vegetable oil (or other high-heat cooking oil)
1 head broccoli, cut into small florets
1 cup snap peas or snow peas
2 carrots, peeled and thinly sliced or julienned
Cooked jasmine or long-grain rice, for serving (approx. 3 cups cooked)
instructions: **Prepare Chicken:** In a medium bowl, combine the chicken pieces with 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tsp rice vinegar, 1/2 tsp sesame oil, and 1 tsp cornstarch. Toss to coat and set aside to marinate for at least 10 minutes (or up to 30 minutes).
**Prepare Stir-fry Sauce:** In a small bowl, whisk together the remaining 1 tbsp soy sauce, 1 tbsp rice vinegar, 1/2 tsp sesame oil, 1 tsp cornstarch, chicken broth, and brown sugar/honey. Set aside.
**Cook Rice:** Prepare rice according to package directions. Keep warm.

**Stir-fry Chicken:** Heat 1 tbsp vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add the marinated chicken and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes, until cooked through and lightly browned. Remove chicken from the pan and set aside.
**Stir-fry Vegetables:** If needed, add a little more oil to the pan. Add sliced onion, minced garlic, and grated ginger. Stir-fry for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add broccoli florets and carrots to the pan. Stir-fry for 3-4 minutes until they start to become tender-crisp.
Add red bell pepper and snap peas. Continue to stir-fry for another 2-3 minutes.

**Combine and Sauce:** Return the cooked chicken to the pan with the vegetables. Give the prepared stir-fry sauce a quick whisk (cornstarch settles at the bottom) and pour it evenly over the chicken and vegetables.
Stir continuously for 1-2 minutes as the sauce thickens and coats all the ingredients.
**Serve:** Remove from heat and serve immediately over hot cooked rice.

oatmeal with berries.name: Oatmeal with Berries
ingredients: 1 cup water or milk (dairy or non-dairy)
1/2 cup fresh or frozen berries (strawberries, blueberries, raspberries, etc.)
1 tablespoon sweetener (honey, maple syrup, sugar, or artificial sweetener, optional)
1 tablespoon nuts or seeds (optional, for topping)
instructions: Combine oats, water or milk, and salt (if using) in a saucepan.
Bring to a boil over medium-high heat, then reduce heat to low and simmer, stirring occasionally, for 5-7 minutes, or until oats are tender and the liquid is mostly absorbed.
If using frozen berries, you can add them during the last 2-3 minutes of cooking to warm them through. If using fresh berries, you can stir them in after cooking or use them as a topping.
Remove from heat and let stand for a minute or two.
Pour oatmeal into a bowl.
Top with berries, sweetener (if desired), nuts or seeds (if using), and a pinch of cinnamon (if desired).
Serve immediately.

tuna salad sandwich.name: Tuna Salad Sandwich
ingredients: Salt and freshly ground black pepper to taste
instructions: In a medium bowl, flake the drained tuna with a fork.
Add mayonnaise, chopped celery, chopped onion, and sweet pickle relish (if using).
Stir in lemon juice (if using).
Season with salt and pepper to taste.
Mix well until all ingredients are combined.
Spread the tuna salad mixture evenly on two slices of bread.
Top with lettuce leaves, if desired.
Cover with the remaining two slices of bread.
Cut sandwiches in half, if desired, and serve.

pasta with tomato sauce and meatballs.name: Pasta with Tomato Sauce and Meatballs
instructions: In a large bowl, combine ground beef, breadcrumbs, Parmesan cheese, beaten egg, minced garlic, chopped parsley, salt, and pepper. Mix well and form into 1-inch meatballs.
Heat olive oil in a large pot or Dutch oven over medium heat. Add meatballs and brown on all sides. Remove meatballs and set aside.
Add chopped onion to the pot and cook until softened, about 5-7 minutes.
Stir in crushed tomatoes, dried basil, dried oregano, and red pepper flakes (if using). Bring to a simmer.
Return meatballs to the pot. Cover and simmer for at least 30 minutes, or until meatballs are cooked through and sauce has thickened.
While the sauce is simmering, cook pasta according to package directions. Drain.
Serve meatballs and sauce over cooked pasta. Garnish with grated Parmesan cheese.
